title: Linkage Fee Nexus Study
summary: The City of Wheat Ridge is soliciting proposals from qualified individuals, institutes, firms, or organizations to complete a Nexus Study for a Housing Impact (Linkage) Fee. The goal is to assess the demand for affordable housing generated by both residential and commercial development and to explore a legally defensible fee structure. The study should evaluate the potential impact on future development activity in Wheat Ridge and whether a linkage fee would be economically viable. This study will directly support the implementation of the City's Affordable Housing Strategy and Action Plan (AHS) by exploring a linkage fee as a secondary revenue source for the City’s Housing Fund. City Council provided this direction based on the results of a consultant-led Inclusionary Housing Zoning (IHZ) Feasibility Analysis, which indicated that an IHZ program may not be the most appropriate tool for Wheat Ridge at this time due to market constraints and development patterns.

summary: REQUEST FOR QUOTATION Information Technology Equipment Clear Creek County, Colorado Issuing Department: Clear Creek County IT Department RFQ Number: RFQ #IT-2026-001 Issue Date: June 9, 2026 Submission Deadline: June 19, 2026 at 5:00 PM MDT Submission Method: Email only — see Section 4 for instructions Contact / Submit To: For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. 1. Purpose and Overview Clear Creek County IT Department is soliciting competitive quotations from qualified vendors for the procurement of IT hardware as itemized in Section 3 of this document. This is an open public request; any qualified vendor is welcome to respond. This RFQ is not a contract or a commitment to purchase. The County reserves the right to accept or reject any or all quotations, to waive any informalities, and to award to the vendor deemed most advantageous to the County. 2. Scope of Work The County is seeking pricing for the following categories of equipment: - One (1) high-performance desktop workstation - Extended warranty / ProSupport upgrade for the workstation - One (1) KVM switch - Two (2) managed network switches Vendors must provide pricing for all line items. Partial bids will not be considered. All equipment must be new, not refurbished or gray-market. 3. Technical Specifications Vendors must quote the exact items specified below, or may propose equivalent substitutions with full specification documentation attached. All substitutions must be clearly identified as such. Item 1 — Desktop Workstation (Qty: 1) Specification Requirement Make / Model Dell Pro Max FCS1250 Desktop Computer (or approved equivalent) Processor Intel Core Ultra 9 285 Memory 32 GB RAM Storage 1 TB SSD Form Factor Slim PC / Small Form Factor Operating System Windows 11 Pro Graphics NVIDIA RTX 2000 Ada — 16 GB GDDR6 Wireless IEEE 802.11be (Wi-Fi 7) Power Supply 360 W Warranty (Base) 3-Year Onsite Warranty Included Accessories 4 x Mini DisplayPort to DisplayPort Adapters Item 2 — Extended Warranty / ProSupport Upgrade (Qty: 1) Specification Requirement Description Dell ProSupport — Extended Warranty Upgrade Coverage Term 5 Years total (upgrade from base 3-year) Response Level Next Business Day (NBD) On-site Coverage Hours 10 x 5 (business hours) Coverage Type Parts & Labor — On-site Maintenance Item 3 — KVM Switch (Qty: 1) Specification Requirement Make / Model StarTech 4-Port USB DisplayPort KVM Switch with Audio, TAA Compliant (or approved equivalent) Ports 4-Port Video Interface DisplayPort (20-pin) USB USB 2.0 Type-A (4-pin, 480 Mbps) Audio 3.5 mm Mini-Jack (3-position) Console Connectors 2 x 3.5 mm Audio Jack, 4 x USB 2.0 Type-A, 1 x DisplayPort Host Connectors 8 x 3.5 mm Audio Jack, 4 x DisplayPort, 4 x USB Type-B Compliance TAA Compliant Item 4 — Managed Network Switch (Qty: 2) Specification Requirement Make / Model Ubiquiti Pro Max Layer 3 Switch — 24 Port (or approved equivalent) Management Managed — Layer 3 Total Ports 24 Ports Port Detail 8 x 2.5 GbE PoE++, 8 x GbE PoE+, 8 x GbE PoE++ Uplinks 2 x 10 Gigabit Ethernet Expansion Slots PoE Budget 400 W Power Consumption 50 W (base) Physical 1U Rack-mountable, Modular Media Type Optical Fiber and Twisted Pair Standards 2.5GBase-T, 10/100/1000Base-T, 10GBase-X 4. Submission Instructions Quotations must be submitted by email no later than June 19, 2026 at 5:00 PM MDT to: For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. Please include the following in your submission: - Subject line: "RFQ #IT-2026-001 — [Your Company Name]" - Itemized pricing for all four (4) line items listed in Section 3 - Unit price and extended price for each item - Shipping cost (if applicable) and estimated delivery timeline - Vendor name, contact name, phone number, and email address - For any proposed equivalents: full technical specifications and documentation 5. Terms and Conditions - This RFQ is open for ten (10) calendar days from the issue date. - All pricing must be valid for a minimum of thirty (30) days from the submission deadline. - Payment terms: Net 30 days from receipt of invoice. - Delivery to: Clear Creek County IT Dept., 405 Argentine Street, Georgetown, CO 80444. - The County is tax-exempt. A tax exemption certificate will be provided upon award. - The County reserves the right to reject any or all quotations and to award based on best value. - Issuance of this RFQ does not obligate Clear Creek County to make a purchase. 6. title: 2026 Sewer Rehabilitation Project
summary: Southgate Sanitation District 2026 Sewer Rehabilitation: Sealed Bids for construction of the 2026 Sewer Rehabilitation will be received by the SOUTHGATE SANITATION DISTRICT (the "Owner") at the office of the District, 3722 East Orchard Road, Centennial, Colorado 80121, and publicly opened and read aloud at 1:00 pm on June 30, 2026. The 2026 Sewer Rehabilitation work generally consists of the following: CIPP (cured-in-place pipe) lining of approximately 1,164-linear feet of 12-inch sewer using UV-cured resin material. CIPP (cured-in-place pipe) lining of approximately 6,244-linear feet of 8-inch sewer using UV-cured resin material OR steam cured material. Both options will be considered for 8-inch CIPP, and contractors are encouraged to provide pricing for each option. Existing sewer pipe materials include vitrified clay pipe (VCP) and concrete pipe. Manholes may be traditional or drop manholes, in various conditions. Rehabilitating thirteen (13), 5-foot diameter, sanitary sewer manholes, where existing stairs are to be removed, interior walls cleaned and latent material removed, inverts raised and reshaped, walls built back with structural cementitious material, active infiltration stopped, and 137-vertical feet of topcoat lining installed. Two (2) manhole rims are to be raised 6-inches above grade and one (1) manhole rim is to be replaced. These are located in unimproved grassed areas. Work will require continuous sewer bypassing, to be completed by the Contractor. The Work will take place throughout the District’s service area, within the City of Centennial and Greenwood Village. All permitting, traffic control, cleaning, protection of the sewers, excavating, asphalt and concrete removal and replacement, CCTV, CIPP lining, sanitary service reactivation, bypass pumping, infiltration stopping, bench build back, cementitious build back, top coating, material and coating testing, surface restoration, site restoration, and abandonments, shall be performed by the Contractor as part of this Work. The Work is expected to be performed in live flow with continuous bypass pumping required. All sanitary sewer flows shall be verified by the Contractor prior to installing and operating the temporary bypass pumping at each location. Contract Documents, including Drawings and Specifications, may be obtained electronically by emailing For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. beginning June 5, 2026. A Bid Bond or certified check payable to the Owner in the amount of 10% of the amount bid must be submitted with each Bid. A virtual pre-bid conference will be held at 1:00 p.m., June 18, 2026. Requests for meeting invite can be made to Erik Vik, PE ( For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. ). Attendance is recommended but not required to submit bid documents. Bids shall be in accordance with the Bid Documents. The successful Bidder will be required to furnish payment and performance bonds required by law for public works projects and provide satisfactory evidence of insurance coverage required by the Contract. The Owner reserves the right to reject any and all Bids and to waive any informalities and irregularities in any Bids.

Scope of work: The complete and total disassembly and removal of log cabin shell and all related building components, contents and materials down to the foundation. Currently, the culvert is undersized for the 100-year storm event, creating the potential for overtopping, piping around the culvert, and eventual washout of the roads. title: Harvest Grass Hay on County-Owned Property
summary: Request for Proposal Harvest Grass Hay on County-Owned Property The Board of County Commissioners intends to contract with a vendor/operator for a three (3) year period with annual renewal to harvest grass hay on county property known as the Deer Creek Meadow. It is estimated that approximately 15-30 tons of hay will be harvested. All bids will be based on a price per ton for all hay harvested and should reflect a fair market value at the time the hay is harvested. BID PREPARATION Interested parties should submit one (1) copy of their bid, no later than October 1, 2024, to the attention of Mike Smith, Interim County Manager. Submittals may be mailed to PO Box 1373, Fairplay, CO 80440, or emailed to For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. . Submittals should contain a cover letter, not exceeding two pages, and concise, informative responses to each of the selection criteria listed below. Other information may be included, as well. EVALUATION CRITERIA The Commissioners will evaluate each bid based on the following factors: - General background of bidding party. - Experience with similar operation contracts. Include references. - What estimated time frame will all the hay be harvested and removed from the property. SELECTION PROCEDURE Based on the information contained in the bid, an interview committee will determine which party could best meet the needs of the County. The interview committee will initiate negotiations with the successful party and make a recommendation to the Board of County Commissioners for approval. The County reserves the right to reject any or all proposals received. Questions regarding this request for proposal (RFP) should be directed to Stevie Wilcox, For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. , or 719-836-4245.
